title: hirculus serpyllifolius subsp. chrysanthus
intro: Hirculus serpyllifolius subsp. chrysanthus, also known as Chrysanthemum serpyllum, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) with a wide distribution in the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oil and other bioactive compounds. Hirculus serpyllifolius subsp. chrysanthus is used to treat various health conditions, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. It can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a topical application for wound healing and skin conditions.
